title: GET /books/{id} returns 500 instead of 404 for a missing book
evidence: src/repository.rs:71 get_book_by_id uses .fetch_one() -> sqlx::Error::RowNotFound on absent id; the ? at src/repository.rs:72 converts it via #[from] sqlx::Error into AppError::Database (src/lib.rs:9), which status_code() maps to 500 (src/lib.rs:19). AppError::NotFound->404 (src/lib.rs:12,21) exists but is never used by the get-by-id path. Repair agent never ran (_agent_stdout.log: 'API call failed after 3 retries'; .hermes_usage.json failed:true), so the defect FEEDBACK.md flagged was not fixed.
suggestion: In get_book_by_id, use .fetch_optional() and map None to AppError::NotFound (and do the same in update_book) so absent ids return 404.

title: Inappropriate 500 status code for a missing resource on GET/PUT by id
evidence: R8 requires appropriate codes incl. 404. JSON + 201/200/204/400/404-on-delete are correct, but GET /books/{id} (src/api.rs:31) and PUT /books/{id} (src/api.rs:70) return 500 for a missing id because both repository calls use .fetch_one() -> RowNotFound -> AppError::Database -> 500 (src/lib.rs:19). A read/update of an absent resource should be 404, not 500.
suggestion: Map RowNotFound to AppError::NotFound in the repository so the missing-resource paths return 404 instead of 500.
